factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments due to acquisitions and program wins.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended July 3, 2020, as compared to the three months ended June 28, 2019, was primarily attributable to $206 million of revenues related to the acquisition of Dynetics, Inc. (''Dynetics'') and program wins, partially offset by the completion of certain contracts, approximately $18 million of negative impacts from reduced volume on certain contracts due to COVID-19 and adverse exchange rate movements, primarily related to changes in the Australian dollar when compared to the U.S. dollar.

factors.summary: Organic growth is stable with some deceleration due to COVID-19 impacts.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating income for the six months ended July 3, 2020, as compared to the six months ended June 28, 2019, was primarily due to approximately $16 million of negative impacts from reduced volume on certain contracts due to COVID-19 and higher amortization of intangible assets related to the acquisition of Dynetics. This was partially offset by program wins and a net increase in volumes on certain programs.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with some increases due to program wins but also declines due to COVID-19.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended July 3, 2020, as compared to the three months ended June 28, 2019, was primarily attributable to $206 million of revenues related to the acquisition of Dynetics, program wins, partially offset by the completion of certain contracts, approximately $18 million of negative impacts from reduced volume on certain contracts due to COVID-19 and adverse exchange rate movements, primarily related to changes in the Australian dollar when compared to the U.S. dollar.

evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with some impact from COVID-19 related costs.

evidence.text: The outbreak has impacted each of our groups, primarily in access to customer sites, travel restrictions, limitations of remote work and COVID-19 related costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable but face some impact from COVID-19 related costs.

factors.summary: Margins are expanding due to lower indirect expenditures and program wins, but may face pressure from COVID-19 related costs.

evidence.text: The increase in operating income for the three months ended July 3, 2020, as compared to the three months ended June 28, 2019, was primarily due to lower indirect expenditures and program wins. This was partially offset by a net decrease in program volumes, including approximately $11 million of negative impacts from reduced volume on certain contracts due to COVID-19.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding due to lower indirect expenditures and program wins, despite some COVID-19 related pressures.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are partially offsetting the impacts of COVID-19.

evidence.text: We have taken measures to protect the health and well-being of our workforce and are working with our customers to minimize the delay and disruption of the award and performance on our contracts. Many of our employees continue to work remotely while our offices remain open with limited capacity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions are partially offsetting the impacts of COVID-19, indicating effective management.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident through increased remote work capabilities.

evidence.text: We have taken steps to support increased teleworking and safe workplace environments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are evident through increased remote work capabilities.

factors.summary: Supply chain is disrupted due to COVID-19, with ongoing challenges in lead times and availability.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 outbreak is affecting major economic and financial markets, and effectively all industries and governments are facing challenges, which has resulted in a period of business disruption, the length and severity of which cannot be predicted.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain is disrupted due to COVID-19, facing ongoing challenges in lead times and availability.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Quality and safety are maintained with no significant incidents reported.

factors.summary: Forward guidance is cautious due to uncertainties from COVID-19.

evidence.text: The full extent of the impact of the COVID-19 outbreak on our operational and financial performance, including our ability to execute on programs in the expected timeframe, will depend on future developments, including the duration and spread of the outbreak and related actions taken by the U.S. government, state and local government officials and international governments to prevent disease spread, all of which are uncertain and cannot be predicted.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company expresses caution due to uncertainties from COVID-19, indicating potential risks ahead.

factors.summary: Management is cautious, with actions taken to mitigate COVID-19 impacts.

evidence.text: We have taken measures to protect the health and well-being of our workforce and are working with our customers to minimize the delay and disruption of the award and performance on our contracts. Many of our employees continue to work remotely while our offices remain open with limited capacity.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious but taking action to mitigate impacts, showing preparedness but not strong optimism.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing disruptions from COVID-19 and potential delays in government spending.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 outbreak is affecting major economic and financial markets, and effectively all industries and governments are facing challenges, which has resulted in a period of business disruption, the length and severity of which cannot be predicted.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include ongoing disruptions and delays, suggesting challenges in the short term.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is stable with focus on core capabilities and market segments.

evidence.text: We are a FORTUNE 500 ® science, engineering and information technology company that provides services and solutions in the defense, intelligence, civil and health markets, both domestically and internationally.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company maintains a stable long-term strategy, but there's no indication of significant positive or negative developments.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include government budgets, COVID-19 impacts, and international trade policies.

evidence.text: Our business performance is affected by the overall level of U.S. government spending, especially on national security, homeland security and intelligence, and the alignment of our service and product offerings and capabilities with current and future budget priorities of the U.S. government.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company identifies multiple sensitivities but does not indicate a clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Catalysts include potential contract reimbursements under the CARES Act and program wins.

evidence.text: We are seeking reimbursement of some of the COVID-19 related costs under our U.S. government contracts through a combination of equitable adjustments to the contract prices and reimbursement of the costs under Section 3610, which allows, but does not require, federal agencies to reimburse contractors at the minimum applicable contract billing rate for costs arising from certain paid leave, including sick leave, a contractor provides to keep its employees or subcontractors in a ready state, including to protect the life and safety of government and contractor personnel, through September 30, 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Potential contract reimbursements and program wins could provide opportunities for growth.

evidence.text: We bring domain-specific capability and cross-market innovations to customers in each of these markets by leveraging seven core capabilities: cyber; digital modernization; integrated systems; mission software systems; mission support; operations and logistics; and sensors, collection and phenomenology.

The following discussion and analysis of Leidos Holdings, Inc.'s financial condition, results of operations, and quantitative and qualitative discussion about business environment and trends should be read in conjunction with Leidos' condensed consolidated financial statements and related notes.
evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive free cash flow supports operations and strategic investments, indicating a strong financial position.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns have been stable, as indicated by consistent dividend payments and share repurchase activities, reflecting the company's confidence in its financial position.

evidence.text: The company has maintained its dividend payments and has a share repurchase program in place, signaling confidence in its financial position.

We paid dividends of $48 million and $99 million during the three and six months ended July 3, 2020, respectively, and $47 million and $101 million during the three and six months ended June 28, 2019, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Consistent dividend payments and share repurchases show confidence in financial position, a positive signal.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active MA activity, with successful integration of acquisitions contributing to growth, as evidenced by increased revenues from the acquisition of Dynetics and the use of capital to fund such activities.

evidence.text: On May 4, 2020, we completed our acquisition of the SD&A Businesses and drew on the Facility in an aggregate principal amount of $1.0 billion.

The increase in revenues for the three months ended July 3, 2020, as compared to the three months ended June 28, 2019, was primarily attributable to $206 million of revenues related to the acquisition of Dynetics, program wins, partially offset by the completion of certain contracts, approximately $18 million of negative impacts from reduced volume on certain contracts due to COVID-19 and adverse exchange rate movements, primarily related to changes in the Australian dollar when compared to the U.S. dollar.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active M&A activity with successful integrations and growth from acquisitions like Dynetics indicates expansion and opportunity.

factors.summary: The company's litigation exposure appears to be stable with no significant new developments, though there have been some legal matters that have been manageable.

evidence.text: The company has faced some legal matters, but the impact has been manageable.
Litigation exposure is stable with no significant new developments noted.
evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

factors.summary: The company has improved its debt leverage through successful refinancing, resulting in a stronger balance sheet and stable debt levels. The refinancing activities have contributed to better financial covenants and manageable debt expenses.

evidence.text: In January 2020, we entered into a Credit Agreement with certain financial institutions, which provided for a senior unsecured term loan facility in an aggregate principal amount of $1.9 billion. We used the proceeds of the Term Loan Facility and cash on hand to repay in full all indebtedness, and terminate all commitments, under, and discharge and release all guarantees and liens existing in connection with the credit agreements entered into in August 2016.

The increase in non-operating expense, net for the three months ended July 3, 2020, was primarily due to $12 million of debt discount and deferred financing costs written off related to the refinancing of our bridge facilities and higher interest expense associated with increases in our long-term debt.

factors.summary: The company's revenue is heavily reliant on U.S. government defense contracts, which make up approximately 87-88% of total revenues. However, the U.S. government fiscal year 2021 budget cycle is expected to be delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic, potentially leading to a re-evaluation of spending levels and priorities.

evidence.text: The U.S. government fiscal year 2021 budget cycle will likely be delayed as the U.S. government focuses its attention on mitigating the impact of COVID-19, which could result in a re-evaluation of U.S. government spending levels and priorities.
U.S. Government Markets During the three and six months ended July 3, 2020, we generated approximately 87% and 88%, respectively, of our total revenues from contracts with the U.S. government.
